name: Cobalt Strike Beacon
categoryDescription: Commonly abused tools that leave NO Windows registry artifacts. These tools are typically single executables, portable builds, or in-memory frameworks. Detection depends entirely on non-registry evidence: process execution logs, prefetch, ShimCache/Amcache, BAM/DAM, network traffic, and memory forensics. Includes detection notes for each tool.
description: Commercial adversary simulation framework. The beacon payload runs entirely in memory by design. No disk artifacts unless operator explicitly stages the payload to disk. No native registry persistence unless the operator configures a persistence mechanism (IFEO, Run key, Service, etc.).
forensic_value: No registry artifacts. IMPORTANT REGISTRY EXCEPTION: Cobalt Strike operators sometimes store beacon configuration or shellcode in registry values. RegRipper 4.0's sizes.pl detects keys with ~760 REG_SZ values (CS staging pattern) and findexes.pl detects MZ PE headers stored in binary registry values.
